Clause:
The vendor, in accordance with ATN INTERNATIONAL, INC.'s 10-Q of November 8, 2019, is expected to adhere to FCC regulations concerning Radio Frequency (RF) radiation safety, including ANSI C95.1-1982 adopted by the FCC in 1986, and 47 CFR 1.1310 RF Maximum Permissible Exposure limits. The vendor is tasked with implementing an RF safety program, but it is understood that workers may not always have complete control over their exposure to RF fields.

The vendor is responsible for creating a safety organization to manage roles and responsibilities, including training content and descriptions, communication channels, inspections and audits, standards and criteria, and the frequency and timing of these aspects of the safety program. However, the vendor need not establish a system for recording and reporting tower crew personnel certificates or required environmental and public health and safety information unless otherwise mandated by federal, state, or local regulations.

The vendor is expected to maintain staff sufficient to provide functional oversight, but they are not required to perform audits to ensure compliance at the national, regional, or market level. In the event of a work-related or OSHA reportable "major incident" (hospital/death), the vendor will endeavor to develop a recovery plan within thirty (30) days. This plan should outline the specific failures that led to the incident and describe any practices that have been or will be implemented to prevent similar incidents in the future.

In cases of conflict between this Subsection's requirements and any vendor requirements imposed on its subcontractors, the health and safety requirements of the vendor shall prevail, unless the subcontractor's requirements are stricter. In such cases, the stricter health and safety requirements of the subcontractor shall apply.
